programmes.show_description: Top moments from the comedy panel show. Jamali Maddix, Stacey Solomon, Ed Gamble, Kerry Godliman, Tom Read Wilson and many more join Jimmy Carr, Rob Beckett and Katherine Ryan.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Jon Richardson and Amy Gledhill take on Richard Ayoade and Guz Khan. Sam Campbell joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Manny Come Home: Fran comes back from holiday to discover that Manny has resigned and is now working next door at Goliath Books. And Bernard has let things slip a little...
Teleshopping
Teleshopping
Undercover bosses Amit Kleinberger, Randy DeWitt and Steve Greenbaum take another look inside their firms, but this time they each send an employee undercover in their place.
Jeff Platt, the president and CEO of a chain of indoor trampoline parks, goes undercover in his own company, to find out whether his fast-growing business is running smoothly.
In Sheffield, there's bickering, failed romance and plenty of revelations, as car salesman Paul and his partner Sara, newly-weds Russell and Shane, and kooky duo Janine and Neil compete.
In Portsmouth, there's organic fare, glitter and sparkle, and some school dinner comparisons, as three couples compete to host the best dinner party.
...Selling: Mike and Steve's cosy cottage is now home to four chaotic children too. They desperately need more room, but will a 200-year-old property be what they're looking for?
The One with the Wannabe King: Harry wants to be king for the day. But bride Charlotte wants something low key. Will this be Harry's crowning moment or will it be 'off with his head'?
The One at a Music Festival: Jake wants the ultimate wedding knees-up, but bride-to-be Rosie dreams of a more intimate affair. Will this festival wedding prove to be a headliner?
Armando Montelongo, CEO of Texas-based Armando Montelongo Companies, one of the leaders in real estate investment education in the US, goes incognito to work beside his staff.
Gina Butler is the founder and CEO of Gigi's Cupcakes, one of America's most successful bakeries, based in Nashville. She dons a disguise to go undercover among her workforce.
Finn McCool's: Gordon Ramsay visits Finn McCool's, a traditional Irish pub in the Hamptons, where the family feuding has got out of hand. Can Gordon mediate?
The Olde Stone Mill: Gordon Ramsay visits The Olde Stone Mill in Tuckahoe, New York. The restaurant has been restored, but owner Dean can't get customers through the door.
...and Selling: Brand new series: Beth and Chris want to move to a better district, but their house is aesthetically challenged - made worse when Jonathan uncovers bad news.
The Bitter End: The final night out goes as smoothly as you'd expect when things heat up as the crew say their final goodbyes.
There are fires, alarms, bizarre banquets and surprising sound effects, as our five comics complete tasks under the watchful eye of Greg Davies.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Harry Hill take on Jon Richardson and Rose Matafeo. Alex Horne and The Horne Section join Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Joe Wilkinson take on Jon Richardson and Kiri Pritchard-McLean. The Brett Domino Trio join Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Quantocking: Mark is worried he's drifting apart from Sophie, so immediate action is called for: he must propose... and where better than the Quantocks?
Sophie's Parents: Mark and Sophie are engaged to be married. Unfortunately Mark is having doubts; crippling doubts.
Alice flushes away Lucy's toilet tatt, Jay tackles Tom's superhero design, Sketch covers Chris's boastful nickname and Glen removes Darren's chat-up line leg art.
There are fires, alarms, bizarre banquets and surprising sound effects, as our five comics complete tasks under the watchful eye of Greg Davies.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Harry Hill take on Jon Richardson and Rose Matafeo. Alex Horne and The Horne Section join Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Teleshopping
Teleshopping
John Hartmann, a former FBI agent and the president and CEO of Chicago-based hardware co-operative True Value, goes incognito among his workforce.
Bill Peduto, the 60th mayor of Pittsburgh, goes incognito among his workforce, to hear from the city's employees and ensure that the changes he's introduced have been effective.
In Cornwall, there are Friesian cows, pitchforks and zombie apocalypse advice, in an eclectic edition of the dining competition.
In Lincolnshire and Yorkshire, there's a one-man comedy show, a posh take on some Yorkshire classics, and a big surprise for one guest.
...Selling: Jeremy and Dayna need some real estate therapy. As the brothers dish out the diagnoses, Dayna realises that even after 10 years, she might not be ready to move.
The One in an Old Mine: It's high noon for impulsive groom Steve as he plans a cliff-hanger of a Wild West wedding for Leona. Will Steve's nuptials on a bridge prove a swinging success?
The One with the Wight Wedding: Groom Liam wants a wedding day that celebrates his homeland, the Isle of Wight. Bride Kandi wants a more standard day. Will it be all-white or go all wrong?
Courtland Gray, chief operating officer of Mississippi-based audio equipment manufacturer Peavey Electronics, goes incognito in his workforce. How do the front-line staff feel?
Todd Pedersen, the founder and CEO of Vivint, a leading solar and home security firm based in Utah, goes undercover among his employees, posing as a reality show contestant.
Giuseppi's: Gordon Ramsay visits a family-owned Italian in Michigan, run by a father and son who constantly clash. Can he get the restaurant - and the family - back on track?
Handlebar: Gordon Ramsay takes on a time warp at the Handlebar restaurant and lounge in Mount Sinai, New York, where the owners are stuck in the 80s with an unenthusiastic chef.
...and Selling: Brand new series: Stuck in real estate limbo, Chandra and Tom have decreased the value of their property. The bros try to raise the bar with an ambitious upgrade.
New Capt, New Rules: Captain Kerry Titheradge takes control of the 197-foot St David as he greets his crew - faces old and new - off the coast of Grenada.
Greg Davies is impressed by Charlotte Ritchie's snack-munching methods and Jamali Maddix's pretty wings, as the race to win Greg's skull-trophy continues.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Richard Ayoade take on Jon Richardson and Katherine Ryan. David O'Doherty joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Miles Jupp take on Jon Richardson and Sophie Duker. Lee and Dean join Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Joe Wilkinson and Rachel Riley.
Conference: At the annual JLB conference in Kettering, Mark's boss, Johnson (Paterson Joseph), tells Mark he has to give a presentation in front of the entire board.
Gym: Mark is still having doubts about marrying Sophie, so joins a gym in order to spend as much time away from her as possible.
